title: Reading and Use of English – Part 1
description: Read the text below and decide which answer (A, B, C or D) best fits each gap.

content.text: The Olympic Games are one of the most (0) enduring legacies of ancient Greek culture. Originally held in honor of Zeus, these athletic competitions (1) place every four years in the sanctuary of Olympia. For the ancient Greeks, the games were not just about sport; they were a (2) of religious and political significance. During the event, a sacred truce was (3) , allowing athletes and spectators to travel safely from warring city-states. This (4) on peace and unity was a central theme of the ancient games. Winners were awarded olive wreaths and were (5) as heroes in their home cities, often receiving lifelong honors. The games continued for over a thousand years before being (6) by the Roman Emperor Theodosius. It was not until the late nineteenth century that the modern Olympics were (7) , thanks to the efforts of Pierre de Coubertin. Today, the games continue to (8) the spirit of international cooperation and excellence. While the scale and nature of the competitions have changed significantly, the core values of friendship and respect remain as relevant today as they were in ancient times, bringing together people from every corner of the globe in a shared celebration of human achievement and athletic prowess.

example.explanation: “Enduring legacy” is a fixed collocation for something that lasts for a long time.

questions.explanation: “Take place” is the standard idiom for an event occurring.
“A matter of significance” is a formal way to describe something important.
“Observed” means to follow or obey a law, custom, or agreement.
“Emphasis on” means giving special importance to something.
“Hailed as” is a common passive structure meaning to be praised as something.
“Abolished” is the formal verb for officially ending a system or practice.
“Revived” means to bring something back to life or use after a long period.
“Embody” means to be a visible or tangible representation of an idea or spirit.
